home_description: This web application serves as an experimental environment for data collection for my bachelor's thesis on the topic of
thesis_topic: Weber-Fechner's law in relation to Malleable Glyphs.
experiment_how_step1: Three glyphs (images) will appear on the screen: A, B, and C (A and C can be in any relative size order).
experiment_how_step2: A and C have a fixed size, you have to adjust B using the mouse wheel, touchpad, or on touch devices by dragging up or down on the middle glyph.
experiment_how_step3: Your task is to set B so that it subjectively corresponds to the
experiment_how_step3_bold: center of sizes (progressions) between A and C.
experiment_how_step4: After clicking "Next", the anonymous results will be saved to a CSV file.
experiment_calibration: For the correct physical size of the glyphs (1x1 inch), I recommend calibrating your monitor/display.
time_required_description: The length of the experiment is not fixed and depends on how much time you dedicate to it. The experiment can be stopped at any time, and any number of completed tasks is beneficial for the research.
thank_you: Thank you very much for your time and willingness to participate!
more_info_description: If you are interested in the topic of Malleable Glyphs and want to learn more about it:
github_repository: GitHub repository of the mglyph library for Python
calibration_description: Adjust the size of the square so that its side corresponds to 1 inch (2.54 cm) on your ruler. This will ensure that the glyphs are rendered at the correct physical size.
exit_confirm: Are you sure you want to exit the experiment?
experiment_done_message: Thank you! The results are being saved to a CSV file for this session.
glyphB_hint: Mouse wheel or dragging up and down
